task_completion.visual_evidence: The Tor Browser window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The browser has navigated to the target website.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The address bar shows 'community.torproject.org' and the webpage is loaded.

task_completion.description: The Certificate Viewer window is open for the target website.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Certificate Viewer' window is open, displaying certificate details for 'community.torproject.org'.

task_completion.description: The certificate chain is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog shows the file being saved as 'target_cert_chain.pem' in the 'Documents' directory, or a file manager shows the file present in /home/ga/Documents.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the Certificate Viewer is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot tool or save dialog shows an image being saved as 'cert_viewer_screenshot.png' in the 'Documents' directory, or a file manager shows the file present in /home/ga/Documents.

integrity.description: The agent used Tor Browser instead of another browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for Tor Browser branding, such as the onion icon in the toolbar or the window title, rather than standard Firefox or Chrome icons.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine browser Certificate Viewer.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Certificate Viewer should be the browser's native graphical interface, not a text editor, terminal output, or mock-up.

integrity.description: The agent used GUI tools to save the files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for browser save dialogs and GUI screenshot utilities rather than terminal windows executing commands like 'curl', 'wget', or 'scrot'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target domain: community.torproject.org. Expected certificate path: /home/ga/Documents/target_cert_chain.pem. Expected screenshot path: /home/ga/Documents/cert_viewer_screenshot.png.
